description_long: This house features a long, rectangular structure with a steeply pitched gable roof adorned with several small chimneys. The facade is characterized by a half-timbered design, with dark wooden beams forming geometric patterns over a light-colored wall surface. The lower portion of the building is constructed from red brick, providing a solid base and visual contrast to the upper stories. Large, evenly spaced windows line the length of the building, arranged in symmetrical rows, allowing ample natural light inside. The entryways are marked by pointed gable awnings above the doors, adding a touch of Gothic Revival influence. The overall appearance suggests a blend of Tudor and traditional European architectural styles, suitable for a residential neighborhood.
description: A long, half-timbered house with a steep gable roof, red brick base, and symmetrical rows of large windows. The facade features pointed gable entryways and a blend of Tudor and traditional European styles.
description_view_0: The end view shows the steep gable roof, half-timbered facade, and red brick base, with pointed gable features above the windows.
description_view_1: The side view reveals the building's full length, symmetrical rows of large windows, and multiple chimneys on the roof.
description_view_2: The opposite end view mirrors view 0, with the same gable roof, half-timbered design, and brick base.
description_view_3: The opposite side view matches view 1, showing the long facade, window arrangement, and roof details.
attribute_entryway_design: Pointed gable awnings above main doors, no porch, and decorative half-timbering around entry.
